idea: Vetted pet sitter matching platform with background checks and real-time updates
targetUser: Dog owners aged 28-55 in urban areas who travel monthly for work, own expensive dogs, and are willing to pay premium for safety
founderContext: I have a Goldendoodle worth 3000 dollars. Last year I used Rover 8 times. 3 times sitters cancelled last minute, 2 times my dog came back dehydrated, 1 time sitter was never background checked. I pay 75 per night now instead of 45 because I only use one trusted sitter I found through a friend. Finding that ONE sitter took 4 months of Rover trials. My dog getting sick cost me 1200 dollars vet bill. The anxiety before every trip ruins the trip itself.
priorResearch: Interviewed 22 dog owners. 19 said they had a bad sitter experience. 17 said they reuse the same 1-2 sitters and avoid new ones because of risk. 16 said they would pay 50 percent more for background-checked sitters. 14 mentioned sitter cancellations as biggest issue. 12 had dogs get sick from sitters. Current solutions: Rover with fees 20 percent too high and no real vetting, Care.com does not vet pet sitters, local Facebook groups are slow and unreliable, word of mouth takes months to find and is expensive through friends. Problem: high-income dog owners cannot find reliable sitters because existing platforms do not properly vet, have no accountability, and allow unreliable sitters to operate. This causes dog health problems, missed trips, anxiety, and overpaying by 50 percent for trusted sitters.
